decode.signature: fn process(&self, ctx: PacketContext) -> Result<Action, DropReason>

short_circuits.description: /localhost packets from non-local faces are rejected
TLV parse failure — invalid wire format
Fragment received, buffered for reassembly — not an error

bytes_ops.note: Interest::decode() gets a clone — same allocation
Each Name component is a zero-copy slice into original buffer

decode.design_note: NDNLPv2 fragment reassembly handled inline. Name is always decoded eagerly; Nonce and Lifetime are behind OnceLock<T> for lazy decode on cache hits.

cs_lookup.signature: fn process(&self, ctx: PacketContext) -> Result<Action, DropReason>

short_circuits.description: Cached Data found — sent directly to consumer, skipping PIT/FIB/strategy entirely

bytes_ops.note: Cache hit returns wire-format Bytes — zero re-encoding, straight to face.send()

cs_lookup.design_note: CS is checked BEFORE PIT. A cache hit satisfies with zero PIT state — no entry created, no cleanup needed. This is the fastest path through the forwarder: single-digit microseconds.

pit_check.signature: fn process(&self, ctx: PacketContext) -> Result<Action, DropReason>

short_circuits.description: Same nonce + same name seen from different face — forwarding loop
PIT entry already exists — Interest aggregated, not forwarded again

pit_check.design_note: DashMap provides sharded concurrent access. Different PIT entries hash to different shards, so operations on unrelated Interests never contend. PitToken is a stable u64 hash computed once at decode.

strategy.signature: fn process(&self, ctx: PacketContext) -> Result<Action, DropReason>

short_circuits.description: No FIB entry matches the Interest name

strategy.design_note: Two-phase: FIB longest-prefix match finds candidates, then strategy selects nexthops. Strategy receives immutable StrategyContext — cannot mutate global state. Default is BestRoute (lowest cost). decide_sync() avoids Box::pin for BestRoute/Multicast.

validation.signature: fn process(&self, ctx: PacketContext) -> Result<Action, DropReason>

short_circuits.description: Signature invalid or trust schema violated

validation.design_note: Always runs when security is enabled. Missing certificates trigger side-channel Interest fetches; pending packets are queued and re-validated on cert arrival. SafeData typestate enforces verification at compile time. Ed25519 verification dominates at 46µs — 20× the rest of the pipeline combined.

pit_match.signature: fn process(&self, ctx: PacketContext) -> Result<Action, DropReason>

short_circuits.description: No PIT entry matches — nobody asked for this Data

pit_match.design_note: Probes all selector combinations (can_be_prefix, must_be_fresh). Collects in-record faces, removes PIT entry atomically via DashMap entry API. Unsolicited Data is a security feature: prevents cache pollution.

cs_insert.signature: fn process(&self, ctx: PacketContext) -> Result<Action, DropReason>

bytes_ops.note: CS stores the same Bytes allocation — wire-format preserved for zero-copy cache hits

cs_insert.design_note: Stores wire-format Bytes, not decoded structs. Future cache hits send stored bytes directly without re-encoding — zero-copy in, zero-copy out. Respects NDNLPv2 CachePolicy and FreshnessPeriod.

short_circuits.description: Strategy gives up — propagate Nack to all in-record consumers
Strategy already initiated retry via ForwardAfter

nack_strategy.design_note: Strategy can retry on alternate nexthops (automatic failover) or propagate the Nack downstream. BestRoute with two nexthops tries the second when the first Nacks.

pit.label: Pending Interest Table

fields.name: out_records
fields.description: Upstream faces Interest was forwarded to

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Nanoseconds since epoch — timing wheel eviction

pit.design_note: DashMap gives lock-free reads on the hot path. PitToken is a stable u64 hash of (name, selector, forwarding_hint) computed once at decode and reused. Interest aggregation: second Interest for same name adds in-record but doesn't forward again.

fib.label: Forwarding Information Base
fib.design_note: Hand-over-hand locking via Arc<RwLock<TrieNode>> per child — concurrent lookups on different branches never contend after diverging from their common ancestor. Longest-prefix match is O(k) where k = name components.

cs.label: Content Store

fields.name: data
fields.description: Wire-format Data packet — zero-copy on hit

cs.design_note: Trait-based: LruCs (byte-bounded LRU, default), ShardedCs (concurrent wrapper), FjallCs (persistent on-disk). Stores wire-format Bytes, not decoded structs — cache hits send directly without re-encoding.

strategy_table.label: Strategy Table
strategy_table.design_note: Per-prefix strategy assignment via longest-prefix match. Default strategy (BestRoute) applies if no entry matches. Strategies receive immutable context — they can read FIB, measurements, and extensions but cannot mutate global state.

measurements.label: Measurements Table
measurements.design_note: Per-name statistics (RTT, loss rate) used by strategies to make informed forwarding decisions. Populated from PIT out-record timestamps when Data satisfies entries.

key_methods.name: recv
key_methods.signature: fn recv(&self) -> impl Future<Output = Result<Bytes, FaceError>> + Send

key_methods.name: send
key_methods.signature: fn send(&self, pkt: Bytes) -> impl Future<Output = Result<(), FaceError>> + Send

faces.design_note: Faces are the I/O boundary of the engine. Every face runs its own Tokio recv task feeding a shared mpsc channel. The single-channel design ensures Interest aggregation works correctly across face types.

context_fields.name: raw_bytes
context_fields.description: Wire-format packet — zero-copy from socket buffer

context_fields.name: name
context_fields.description: Parsed NDN name — always decoded eagerly

context_fields.name: name_hashes
context_fields.description: Cumulative prefix hashes — reused by PIT/FIB to avoid re-hashing

context_fields.name: pit_token
context_fields.description: Stable u64 hash for PIT entry lookup

context_fields.name: out_faces
context_fields.description: Selected nexthop faces (strategy) or in-record faces (PIT match)

context_fields.name: cs_hit
context_fields.description: True if Content Store had a match

context_fields.name: arrival
context_fields.description: Monotonic nanosecond timestamp at packet arrival

actions.description: Fan Data back to in-record faces

dispatcher.label: Pipeline Runner
dispatcher.description: Drains inbound packets in batches (up to 64), runs fragment sieve inline, routes to Interest/Data/Nack paths. Configurable: single-threaded (inline) or parallel (tokio::spawn per packet).
dispatcher.design_note: Single mpsc channel from all faces. Batch drain amortizes tokio::select! wakeup cost — 64 packets share one wakeup, reducing scheduling overhead by up to 60x under load.

channels.label: Inbound Channel
channels.design_note: All face types feed one channel — ensures Interest aggregation works across face types. Bounded queue provides backpressure.

channels.label: Per-Face Send Queue
channels.design_note: One per face. Bounded (default 2048). try_send drops on full (congestion backpressure). Per-face ordering preserved (critical for TCP framing).

engine.design_note: Engine as library — no daemon/client split. ForwarderEngine wraps Arc<EngineInner> so cloning is cheap. All shared tables are Arc-wrapped for concurrent access. Shutdown via cooperative CancellationToken.

keychain.label: KeyChain

fields.name: mgr
fields.description: Shared across all tasks — single source of truth

constructors.name: ephemeral()
constructors.description: In-memory Ed25519, self-signed 365-day cert, registered as trust anchor. Tests and short-lived producers.

constructors.name: open_or_create()
constructors.description: File-backed PIB. Generates Ed25519 on first run, reloads on subsequent runs. Production use.

constructors.name: from_parts()
constructors.description: Framework escape hatch for NDNCERT enrollment and device provisioning.

keychain.design_note: Single entry point for NDN security. Vends Arc<dyn Signer> so signing state is shared across tasks without cloning keys.

signers.label: Signers

key_methods.name: sign
key_methods.signature: fn sign<'a>(&'a self, region: &'a [u8]) -> BoxFuture<'a, Result<Bytes, TrustError>>

key_methods.name: sign_sync
key_methods.description: Sync fast path — CPU-only signers override to avoid Box::pin

implementations.name: Ed25519Signer
implementations.design_note: sign() just calls sign_sync() — no async overhead for elliptic curve math

implementations.name: Blake3Signer
implementations.design_note: Unauthenticated digest. Dispatches to update_rayon for ≥128KiB. SignatureType code 6

implementations.name: Blake3KeyedSigner
implementations.design_note: Keyed authentication. SignatureType code 7. Distinct code prevents downgrade attacks

signers.design_note: sign_sync() avoids Box::pin heap allocation for CPU-only signers (Ed25519, HMAC, Blake3). Only truly async signers (hardware TPM, remote KMS) need the boxed path.

validator.label: Validator

fields.name: schema
fields.description: Readers (hot path) shared lock; writers (mgmt API) exclusive lock

fields.name: cert_cache
fields.description: DashMap dual-index: by name + by SHA-256 digest

fields.name: max_chain
fields.description: Maximum certificate chain depth before rejection

chain_walk: Extract KeyLocator from Data (name or key digest)
Trust schema check: data_name + key_name match a rule
Loop until trust anchor or max depth:
→ If miss: spawn CertFetcher Interest, return Pending
→ If hit: verify signature with cert's public key
→ Move to issuer key, detect cycles via HashSet
Trust anchor reached → verify final sig → return SafeData
validator.design_note: Schema stored in Arc<RwLock<TrustSchema>>. Reads (hot validation path) acquire shared lock for ~µs. Writes (management API) acquire exclusive lock — no rebuild needed, rules take effect atomically.

cert_cache.label: Certificate Cache
cert_cache.design_note: Both DashMaps populated on every insert(). Lookups handle both KeyLocator forms without linear search. Sharded for concurrent access.

safe_data.label: SafeData

trust_paths.description: Full certificate chain names from data to trust anchor
Trusted via OS-level SO_PEERCRED on Unix socket

safe_data.design_note: pub(crate) fields make SafeData unconstructable outside ndn-security. The compiler enforces that code expecting verified data cannot receive unverified data — the type IS the proof.

trust_schema.label: Trust Schema

pattern_types.meaning: Captures one component into named variable
Captures all remaining components (must be last)

trust_schema.design_note: Capture variables must be consistent across data and key patterns. A few rules express hierarchical trust, scope restriction, and role-based signing.

performance.label: PacketContext by Value
performance.what: PacketContext is passed by value (move) through PipelineStage::process(). Stages that short-circuit simply don't return the context.
performance.why: Rust's ownership system prevents use-after-hand-off at compile time. No runtime check needed — if it compiles, the packet lifecycle is correct.

performance.label: bytes::Bytes Zero-Copy
performance.what: raw_bytes: Bytes — reference-counted, zero-copy buffer from kernel socket through to CS storage and face.send().
performance.why: A packet passes through 6+ stages without a single memcpy of the wire bytes. CS stores wire-format Bytes; cache hits send directly without re-encoding.

performance.label: SmallVec Inlining
performance.what: out_faces: SmallVec<[FaceId; 4]> — stack-allocated for ≤4 nexthops. nonces_seen: SmallVec<[u32; 4]> — stack-allocated for ≤4 nonces per PIT entry.
performance.why: Typical Interest has 1-2 nexthops, typical PIT entry sees 1 nonce. SmallVec keeps common case on stack (no heap alloc), spills only for unusual scenarios. Array lookup stays in L1 cache.

performance.label: DashMap Sharded Locking
performance.what: PIT uses DashMap — internally a fixed number of RwLock<HashMap> shards. Different PIT entries hash to different shards.
performance.why: Operations on unrelated Interests never contend. N cores process N unrelated packets in parallel with zero lock contention. with_entry() closure holds lock only during callback.

performance.label: Hand-Over-Hand FIB Locking
performance.what: FIB NameTrie: Arc<RwLock<TrieNode>> per child. LPM grabs child Arc, releases parent lock, descends. Explicit drop() before each level.
performance.why: Concurrent lookups on different subtrees never contend after diverging from common ancestor. Only the current node is locked at any moment.

performance.label: decide_sync() Fast Path
performance.what: ErasedStrategy::decide_sync() returns Option<SmallVec<[ForwardingAction; 2]>>. Called first; only falls through to async path if None.
performance.why: BestRoute and Multicast compute decisions synchronously — skip Box::pin heap allocation entirely. Only strategies needing async I/O (cert fetch) use the boxed path. Saves ~50ns per packet.

performance.label: 64-Packet Batch Drain
performance.what: const BATCH_SIZE: usize = 64. One blocking recv(), then up to 63 non-blocking try_recv() calls. Fragment sieve runs inline on batch before spawning tasks.
performance.why: Amortizes tokio::select! wakeup cost across 64 packets. Under load, reduces per-packet scheduling overhead by up to 60x. Fragment sieve at ~2µs/packet runs inline to avoid pipeline entry for fragments.

performance.label: Signer::sign_sync() Override
performance.what: CPU-only signers (Ed25519, HMAC, Blake3) override sign_sync() to avoid Box::pin and async state machine overhead.
performance.why: Ed25519 signing is ~100µs of pure CPU. Boxing a Future adds ~50ns + heap allocation. sign_sync() eliminates both for the common path.
performance.annotation: No async overhead for CPU crypto

bytes_lifecycle.description: Lifecycle of a single Bytes buffer from socket recv through to face send. No semantic copies on the happy path — only ref-count increments via Bytes::clone() and zero-copy views via Bytes::slice().

context_evolution.description: PacketContext fields light up as the packet traverses pipeline stages. Passed by value (move semantics) — a stage that short-circuits simply doesn't return the context.

interest_path.note: PitToken = stable u64 hash, computed once

tasks.description: Tokio task topology. Each task is a long-lived or short-lived async function spawned via tasks.spawn(). Communication via mpsc channels; shared state via DashMap/Arc/RwLock.

permanent.label: Pipeline Runner
permanent.design_note: Single task drains all inbound packets. Batch of up to 64, then spawns per-packet tasks if pipeline_threads > 1, else processes inline.

permanent.label: PIT Expiry
permanent.design_note: Timing-wheel based. O(1) insertion and expiry notification — no periodic scan of the full table.

permanent.label: RIB → FIB Recompute
permanent.design_note: Recomputes FIB from RIB on expiry. Separate from hot-path FIB reads.

permanent.label: Validation Drain
permanent.design_note: Re-validates queued packets whose certificates arrived. Injects back into CS insert stage.

permanent.label: Idle Face Sweep
permanent.design_note: Removes OnDemand faces with no activity for 5 minutes.

permanent.label: Discovery Tick
permanent.design_note: Invokes discovery protocol's periodic handler (hello, service announcements).

per_face.label: Face Reader
per_face.design_note: One per face. Blocks on recv(). On error: Permanent retries, OnDemand cleans up. Non-blocking try_send — NDN-correct to drop on overload.

per_face.label: Face Sender
per_face.design_note: One per face. Sequential send preserves per-face ordering (critical for TCP framing). Reliability layer fragments, assigns TxSeq, piggybacks Acks.

dynamic.label: Per-Packet Task
dynamic.design_note: Spawned by pipeline runner for each complete packet in parallel mode. Up to pipeline_threads active simultaneously.

dynamic.label: Certificate Fetch
dynamic.design_note: Spawned when validation needs a certificate not in cache. Sends Interest for cert, inserts response into CertCache.

channels.design_note: All face types feed ONE channel. Ensures Interest aggregation works across face types.
One per face. try_send() drops on full = congestion backpressure. Per-face ordering preserved.

cancellation.shutdown: ShutdownHandle::shutdown() cancels root → all children cancelled → tasks.join_all()
